blurb: Tall, upright native prairie grass with clouds of airy pink-tinged seed panicles, fiery gold-to-burgundy fall color, and stiff tan stems that hold structure all winter.
water: Low to moderate; remarkably adaptable from wet to dry
spread: Clumping with short rhizomes; can self-sow
seasons: Upright blue-green to green in summer; airy pink-tinged panicles late summer; gold-to-burgundy fall; stiff tan winter structure
wildlife: Larval host for skippers; seeds feed sparrows and other birds; nesting and winter cover
toxic: Can cause photosensitization (saponin hepatotoxicity) in grazing livestock, especially sheep, goats, and horses on young growth; not a concern for people or as an ornamental
